revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Poem quote/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Poem quote/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Poem quote/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Poem quote/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Poem quote/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Poem quote/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} A template for quoting poems, song lyrics, and other things that have frequent line breaks and other things that would generally be ignored in standard wiki formatting. Based on {{tl|Blockquote}}; see there for further usage details. ==Example== {{Markup| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }}| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Compare with {{tl|Blockquote}}: {{Markup| {{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} |{{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Note that while the version above using {{tl|Blockquote}} may appear to work in some browsers, it does not do so consistently in all of them. Use {{tag|poem}} – or a template like {{tlx|Poem quote}} that implements it – around material that requires that whitespace formatting be preserved as in the original, such as poems and interlinear glosses. Due to limitations of how MediaWiki parses wikimarkup and HTML, attempts to do this by just inserting blank lines and extra spaces will not work. This is true of all block elements. (Technical note: Although {{tl|Poem quote}} uses the Tag:#poem markup rather than using {{tag|poem|o}}, it is functionally the same.) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Adds a block quotation.", "params": { "text": { "label": "text", "description": "The text to quote", "type": "content", "required": true, "aliases": [ "1", "quote" ] }, "char": { "label": "char", "description": "The character being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "example": "Alice", "suggested": true }, "sign": { "label": "sign", "description": "The person being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "2", "cite", "author" ], "example": "Lewis Carroll ", "suggested": true }, "title": { "label": "title", "description": "The title of the poem being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "3" ], "example": "Jabberwocky", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "source", "description": "A source for the quote", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "4" ], "example": "Through the Looking-Glass, and What Alice Found There", "suggested": true }, "style": { "label": "style", "description": "Standard CSS style", "type": "string", "required": false } } } ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Quotation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} A template for quoting poems, song lyrics, and other things that have frequent line breaks and other things that would generally be ignored in standard wiki formatting. Based on {{tl|Blockquote}}; see there for further usage details. ==Example== {{Markup| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }}| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Compare with {{tl|Blockquote}}: {{Markup| {{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} |{{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Note that while the version above using {{tl|Blockquote}} may appear to work in some browsers, it does not do so consistently in all of them. Use {{tag|poem}} – or a template like {{tlx|Poem quote}} that implements it – around material that requires that whitespace formatting be preserved as in the original, such as poems and interlinear glosses. Due to limitations of how MediaWiki parses wikimarkup and HTML, attempts to do this by just inserting blank lines and extra spaces will not work. This is true of all block elements. (Technical note: Although {{tl|Poem quote}} uses the Tag:#poem markup rather than using {{tag|poem|o}}, it is functionally the same.) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Adds a block quotation.", "params": { "text": { "label": "text", "description": "The text to quote", "type": "content", "required": true, "aliases": [ "1", "quote" ] }, "char": { "label": "char", "description": "The character being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "example": "Alice", "suggested": true }, "sign": { "label": "sign", "description": "The person being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "2", "cite", "author" ], "example": "Lewis Carroll ", "suggested": true }, "title": { "label": "title", "description": "The title of the poem being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "3" ], "example": "Jabberwocky", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "source", "description": "A source for the quote", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "4" ], "example": "Through the Looking-Glass, and What Alice Found There", "suggested": true }, "style": { "label": "style", "description": "Standard CSS style", "type": "string", "required": false } } } ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Quotation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} A template for quoting poems, song lyrics, and other things that have frequent line breaks and other things that would generally be ignored in standard wiki formatting. Based on {{tl|Blockquote}}; see there for further usage details. ==Example== {{Markup| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }}| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Compare with {{tl|Blockquote}}: {{Markup| {{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} |{{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Note that while the version above using {{tl|Blockquote}} may appear to work in some browsers, it does not do so consistently in all of them. Use {{tag|poem}} – or a template like {{tlx|Poem quote}} that implements it – around material that requires that whitespace formatting be preserved as in the original, such as poems and interlinear glosses. Due to limitations of how MediaWiki parses wikimarkup and HTML, attempts to do this by just inserting blank lines and extra spaces will not work. This is true of all block elements. (Technical note: Although {{tl|Poem quote}} uses the Tag:#poem markup rather than using {{tag|poem|o}}, it is functionally the same.) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Adds a block quotation.", "params": { "text": { "label": "text", "description": "The text to quote", "type": "content", "required": true, "aliases": [ "1", "quote" ] }, "char": { "label": "char", "description": "The character being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "example": "Alice", "suggested": true }, "sign": { "label": "sign", "description": "The person being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "2", "cite", "author" ], "example": "Lewis Carroll ", "suggested": true }, "title": { "label": "title", "description": "The title of the poem being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "3" ], "example": "Jabberwocky", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "source", "description": "A source for the quote", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "4" ], "example": "Through the Looking-Glass, and What Alice Found There", "suggested": true }, "style": { "label": "style", "description": "Standard CSS style", "type": "string", "required": false } } } ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Quotation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} A template for quoting poems, song lyrics, and other things that have frequent line breaks and other things that would generally be ignored in standard wiki formatting. Based on {{tl|Blockquote}}; see there for further usage details. ==Example== {{Markup| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }}| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Compare with {{tl|Blockquote}}: {{Markup| {{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} |{{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Note that while the version above using {{tl|Blockquote}} may appear to work in some browsers, it does not do so consistently in all of them. Use {{tag|poem}} – or a template like {{tlx|Poem quote}} that implements it – around material that requires that whitespace formatting be preserved as in the original, such as poems and interlinear glosses. Due to limitations of how MediaWiki parses wikimarkup and HTML, attempts to do this by just inserting blank lines and extra spaces will not work. This is true of all block elements. (Technical note: Although {{tl|Poem quote}} uses the Tag:#poem markup rather than using {{tag|poem|o}}, it is functionally the same.) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Adds a block quotation.", "params": { "text": { "label": "text", "description": "The text to quote", "type": "content", "required": true, "aliases": [ "1", "quote" ] }, "char": { "label": "char", "description": "The character being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "example": "Alice", "suggested": true }, "sign": { "label": "sign", "description": "The person being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "2", "cite", "author" ], "example": "Lewis Carroll ", "suggested": true }, "title": { "label": "title", "description": "The title of the poem being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "3" ], "example": "Jabberwocky", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "source", "description": "A source for the quote", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "4" ], "example": "Through the Looking-Glass, and What Alice Found There", "suggested": true }, "style": { "label": "style", "description": "Standard CSS style", "type": "string", "required": false } } } ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Quotation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23525408989|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} A template for quoting poems, song lyrics, and other things that have frequent line breaks and other things that would generally be ignored in standard wiki formatting. Based on {{tl|Blockquote}}; see there for further usage details. ==Example== {{Markup| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }}| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Compare with {{tl|Blockquote}}: {{Markup| {{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} |{{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Note that while the version above using {{tl|Blockquote}} may appear to work in some browsers, it does not do so consistently in all of them. Use {{tag|poem}} – or a template like {{tlx|Poem quote}} that implements it – around material that requires that whitespace formatting be preserved as in the original, such as poems and interlinear glosses. Due to limitations of how MediaWiki parses wikimarkup and HTML, attempts to do this by just inserting blank lines and extra spaces will not work. This is true of all block elements. (Technical note: Although {{tl|Poem quote}} uses the Tag:#poem markup rather than using {{tag|poem|o}}, it is functionally the same.) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Adds a block quotation.", "params": { "text": { "label": "text", "description": "The text to quote", "type": "content", "required": true, "aliases": [ "1", "quote" ] }, "char": { "label": "char", "description": "The character being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "example": "Alice", "suggested": true }, "sign": { "label": "sign", "description": "The person being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "2", "cite", "author" ], "example": "Lewis Carroll ", "suggested": true }, "title": { "label": "title", "description": "The title of the poem being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "3" ], "example": "Jabberwocky", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "source", "description": "A source for the quote", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "4" ], "example": "Through the Looking-Glass, and What Alice Found There", "suggested": true }, "style": { "label": "style", "description": "Standard CSS style", "type": "string", "required": false } } } ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Quotation templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24957526818|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} A template for quoting poems, song lyrics, and other things that have frequent line breaks and other things that would generally be ignored in standard wiki formatting. Based on {{tl|Blockquote}}; see there for further usage details. ==Example== {{Markup| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }}| {{Poem quote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Compare with {{tl|Blockquote}}: {{Markup| {{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} |{{Blockquote |text=The sun was shining on the sea, Shining with all his might: He did his very best to make The billows smooth and bright-- And this was odd, because it was The middle of the night. |char=Tweedledum and Tweedledee |sign=Lewis Carroll |source=''Through the Looking-Glass'' |title="The Walrus and The Carpenter" |style= }} }} Note that while the version above using {{tl|Blockquote}} may appear to work in some browsers, it does not do so consistently in all of them. Use {{tag|poem}} – or a template like {{tlx|Poem quote}} that implements it – around material that requires that whitespace formatting be preserved as in the original, such as poems and interlinear glosses. Due to limitations of how MediaWiki parses wikimarkup and HTML, attempts to do this by just inserting blank lines and extra spaces will not work. This is true of all block elements. (Technical note: Although {{tl|Poem quote}} uses the Tag:#poem markup rather than using {{tag|poem|o}}, it is functionally the same.) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Adds a block quotation.", "params": { "text": { "label": "text", "description": "The text to quote", "type": "content", "required": true, "aliases": [ "1", "quote" ] }, "char": { "label": "char", "description": "The character being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "example": "Alice", "suggested": true }, "sign": { "label": "sign", "description": "The person being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "2", "cite", "author" ], "example": "Lewis Carroll ", "suggested": true }, "title": { "label": "title", "description": "The title of the poem being quoted", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "3" ], "example": "Jabberwocky", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "source", "description": "A source for the quote", "type": "content", "required": false, "aliases": [ "4" ], "example": "Through the Looking-Glass, and What Alice Found There", "suggested": true }, "style": { "label": "style", "description": "Standard CSS style", "type": "string", "required": false } } } ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Quotation templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
